narrative:
The stress score has risen from 18 to 25, a meaningful single-session shift driven by continued equity softness, with SPY down 2.0% over five days at a z-score of -1.11 and four of eleven sectors in the red. Credit markets remain composed, with high-yield spreads at 284 basis points and investment-grade at 77 basis points both carrying negative z-scores, suggesting the equity pressure has not yet translated into broader risk-off positioning.

The most notable development today is FXI extending its five-day loss to -6.4% while adding another -1.4% in today's session, pointing to persistent and accelerating selling pressure in Chinese equities that stands out against an otherwise mixed global picture. A break of high-yield spreads toward a positive z-score, or FXI losses broadening into wider EM deterioration beyond the current four of eight markets in the red, would be the clearest trigger for upgrading the regime assessment toward elevated stress.
